notes: Case A is a real StrategyBrief from the Searchable Groq run. proof_point matches ProductKnowledge verbatim and narrative_arc matches ContentBrief — both cross-schema contracts pass. Cases B and C are synthetic edge cases retained for unit testing.

expected_output.lead_pain_point: Difficulty tracking brand mentions and rankings across multiple AI engines daily
expected_output.primary_claim: Searchable surfaces where your brand appears — and where it doesn't — across every major AI engine

test_cases.validation_note: proof_point matches ProductKnowledge.proof_points[0].text verbatim — Contract 1 passes. narrative_arc matches ContentBrief.narrative_arc — Contract 2 passes. lead_pain_point is specific daily friction (tracking across multiple engines), not a generic statement like 'brand visibility is hard'.

product_knowledge_proof_points: Used by Vercel, Raycast, and Mercury teams

expected_output.lead_pain_point: Sprint planning meetings that exist only to copy ticket statuses into Slack
expected_output.primary_claim: Linear removes the status update layer by keeping issue state, git, and comms in one place
expected_output.proof_point: Used by Vercel, Raycast, and Mercury teams

test_cases.validation_note: Synthetic case. proof_point must be drawn from the product_knowledge_proof_points list exactly — not paraphrased. If the model wrote 'trusted by teams like Vercel and Raycast' that would be a Contract 1 violation.

test_cases.source: synthetic — failure case, fabricated proof point

expected_output.lead_pain_point: AI search tools are deciding who gets found — and most brands have no idea where they rank
expected_output.primary_claim: Searchable tracks your brand's AI search presence and shows you how to improve it
expected_output.proof_point: INVALID — model wrote '4x more AI search visibility' which does not appear in proof_points

test_cases.validation_note: This is a Contract 1 failure. proof_point must be rejected at validation because '4x more AI search visibility' does not appear verbatim in ProductKnowledge.proof_points. The Evaluator should score accuracy=1 and return a revision_hint telling the agent to use '206% share of voice improvements' instead.
